evals.prompt: What's our NIST AI RMF GOVERN-6.2 coverage — which entries map to it?
evals.expected_output: Knows NIST AI RMF applies to controls only; searches for GOVERN-6.2@1.0; lists the controls mapped to it (ids + titles).
expectations: Recognizes NIST AI RMF maps to controls only (not risks/personas)
Searches for the version-pinned value GOVERN-6.2@1.0 and lists the controls that carry it, with ids

evals.prompt: Which risks map to MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051?
evals.expected_output: Knows ATLAS techniques (AML.T####) appear on risks; searches AML.T0051@5.0.1; lists the risks (or reports none).
expectations: Recognizes AML.T#### techniques map to risks (mitigations AML.M#### map to controls)
Searches the version-pinned AML.T0051@5.0.1 and lists the risks that carry it (or reports a gap if none)

evals.prompt: Show me our EU AI Act Article 14 coverage.
evals.expected_output: Knows EU AI Act maps to controls + personas; searches Article 14@2024; if nothing maps, reports the coverage gap plainly rather than inventing mappings.
expectations: Recognizes EU AI Act applies to controls and personas (not risks)
Searches for Article 14@2024 across controls.yaml and personas.yaml
If no entries map, reports the coverage gap explicitly (does not invent or infer mappings)

evals.prompt: Is the STRIDE value on riskDataPoisoning formatted correctly and version-pinned?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is a mapping-correctness/style question, not a coverage query; redirects to audit-framework-mappings.
expectations: Recognizes checking mapping format/correctness is out of scope for this coverage skill
Does not attempt a style audit itself
